verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He came unto His own, and His own received Him not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If ye remember our former reflections, we shall the more zealously proceed with the building up of what remains, as doing so for great gain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For so will our discourse be more intelligible to you who remember what has been already said, and we shall not need much labor, because you are able through your great love of learning to see more clearly into what remains.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The man who is always losing what is given to him will always need a teacher, and will never know anything; but he who retains what he has received, and so receives in addition what remains, will quickly be a teacher instead of a learner, and useful not only to himself, but to all others also; as, conjecturing from their great readiness to hear, I anticipate that this assembly will specially be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Come then, let us lay up in your souls, as in a safe treasury, the Lord’s money, and unfold, as far as the grace of the Spirit may afford us power, the words this day set before us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He (St. John) had said, speaking of the old times, that “the world knew him not” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 10 ); afterwards he comes down in his narrative to the times of the proclamation (of the Gospel), and says, “He came to His own, and His own received Him not,” now calling the Jews “His own,” as His peculiar people, or perhaps even all mankind, as created by Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And as above, when perplexed at the folly of the many, and ashamed of our common nature, he said that “the world by Him was made,” and having been made, did not recognize its Maker; so here again, being troubled beyond bearing at the stupidity of the Jews and the many, he sets forth the charge in a yet more striking manner, saying, that “His own received Him not,” and that too when “He came to them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And not only he, but the prophets also, wondering, said the very same, as did afterwards Paul, amazed at the very same things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus did the prophets cry aloud in the person of Christ, saying, “A people whom I have not known, have served Me; as soon as they heard Me, they obeyed Me; the strange children have dealt falsely with Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The strange children have waxed aged, and have halted from their paths.” ( Ps. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, “They to whom it had not been told concerning Him, shall see, and they which had not heard, shall understand.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And, “I was found of them that sought Me not” ( Isa. lii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 15 ); “I was made manifest unto them that asked not after me.” ( Isa. xlv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 1, as quoted Rom. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Paul, in his Epistles to the Romans, has said, “What then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Israel hath not obtained that which he seeketh for: but the election hath obtained it.” ( Rom. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again; “What shall we say then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That the Gentiles which followed not after righteousness, have attained unto righteousness: but Israel which followed after the law of righteousness, hath not attained to the law of righteousness.” ( Rom. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is a thing indeed worthy of our amazement, how they who were nurtured in (knowledge of) the prophetical books, who heard Moses every day telling them ten thousand things concerning the coming of the Christ, and the other prophets afterwards, who moreover themselves beheld Christ Himself daily working miracles among them, giving up His time to them alone, neither as yet allowing His disciples to depart into the way of the Gentiles, or to enter into a city of Samaritans, nor doing so Himself, but everywhere declaring that He was sent to the lost sheep of the house of Israel ( Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 5 ): how, (I say), while they saw the signs, and heard the Prophets, and had Christ Himself continually putting them in remembrance, they yet made themselves once for all so blind and dull, as by none of these things to be brought to faith in Christ. ( Matt. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: While they of the Gentiles, who had enjoyed none of these things, who had never heard the oracles of God, not, as one may say, so much as in a dream, but ever ranging among the fables of madmen, (for heathen philosophy is this,) having ever in their hands the sillinesses of their poets, nailed to stocks and stones, and neither in doctrines nor in conversation possessing anything good or sound.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: (For their way of life was more impure and more accursed than their doctrine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As was likely; for when they saw their gods delighting in all wickedness, worshiped by shameful words, and more shameful deeds, reckoning this festivity and praise, and moreover honored by foul murders, and child-slaughters, how should not they emulate these things?)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still, fallen as they were as low as the very depth of wickedness, on a sudden, as by the agency of some machine, they have appeared to us shining from on high, and from the very summit of heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then and whence came it to pass?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear Paul telling you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that blessed person searching exactly into these things, ceased not until he had found the cause, and had declared it to all others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then is it? and whence came such blindness upon the Jews?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear him who was entrusted with this stewardship declare.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then does he say in resolving this doubt of the many? ( 1 Cor. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For they,” says he, “being ignorant of God’s righteousness and going about to establish their own righteousness, have not submitted themselves unto the righteousness of God.” ( Rom. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore they have suffered this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, explaining the same matter in other terms, he says, “What shall we say then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That the Gentiles which followed not after righteousness, have attained unto righteousness, even the righteousness which is of faith; but Israel, which followed after the law of righteousness, hath not attained to the law of righteousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because they sought it not by faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they stumbled at that stumbling stone.” ( Rom. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: His meaning is this: “These men’s unbelief has been the cause of their misfortunes, and their haughtiness was parent of their unbelief.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when having before enjoyed greater privileges than the heathen, through having received the law, through knowing God, and the rest which Paul enumerates, they after the coming of Christ saw the heathen and themselves called on equal terms through faith, and after faith received one of the circumcision in nothing preferred to the Gentile, they came to envy and were stung by their haughtiness, and could not endure the unspeakable and exceeding lovingkindness of the Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So this has happened to them from nothing else but pride, and wickedness, and unkindness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For in what, O most foolish of men, are ye injured by the care bestowed on others?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How are your blessings made less through having others to share the same?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But of a truth wickedness is blind, and cannot readily perceive anything that it ought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Being therefore stung by the prospect of having others to share the same confidence, they thrust a sword against themselves, and cast themselves out from the lovingkindness of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And with good reason.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He saith, “Friend, I do thee no wrong, I will give to ‘these also’ even as unto thee.” ( Matt. xx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or rather, these Jews are not deserving even of these words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the man in the parable if he was discontented, could yet speak of the labors and weariness, the heat and sweat, of a whole day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what could these men have to tell? nothing like this, but slothfulness and profligacy and ten thousand evil things of which all the prophets continued ever to accuse them, and by which they like the Gentiles had offended against God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Paul declaring this says, “For there is no difference between the Jew and the Greek: For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God: being justified freely by His grace.” ( Rom. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 12; Rom. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And on this head he treats profitably and very wisely throughout that Epistle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But in a former part of it he proves that they are worthy of still greater punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For as many as have sinned in the law shall be judged by the law” ( Rom. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 12 ); that is to say, more severely, as having for their accuser the law as well as nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And not for this only, but for that they have been the cause that God is blasphemed among the Gentiles: “My Name,” He saith, “is blasphemed among the Gentiles through you.” ( Rom. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 24; Isa. lii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since now this it was that stung them most, (for the thing appeared incredible even to those of the circumcision who believed, and therefore they brought it as a charge against Peter, when he was come up to them from Cesarea, that he “went in to men uncircumcised, and did eat with them” ( Acts xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3 ); and after that they had learned the dispensation of God, even so still they wondered how “on the Gentiles also was poured out the gift of the Holy Ghost” ( Acts x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 45 ): showing by their astonishment that they could never have expected so incredible a thing,) since then he knew that this touched them nearest, see how he has emptied their pride and relaxed their highly swelling insolence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For after having discoursed on the case of the heathen, and shown that they had not from any quarter any excuse, or hope of salvation, and after having definitely charged them both with the perversion of their doctrines and the uncleanness of their lives, he shifts his argument to the Jews; and after recounting all the expressions of the Prophet, in which he had said that they were polluted, treacherous, hypocritical persons, and had “altogether become unprofitable,” that there was “none” among them “that seeketh after God,” that they had “all gone out of the way” ( Rom. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 12 ), and the like, he adds, “Now we know that what things soever the law saith, it saith to them who are under the law: that every mouth may be stopped, and all the world may become guilty before God.” ( Rom. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God.” ( Rom. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then exaltest thou thyself, O Jew? why art thou high minded? for thy mouth also is stopped, thy boldness also is taken away, thou also with all the world art become guilty, and, like others, art placed in need of being justified freely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou oughtest surely even if thou hadst stood upright and hadst had great boldness with God, not even so to have envied those who should be pitied and saved through His lovingkindness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is the extreme of wickedness, to pine at the blessings of others; especially when this was to be effected without any loss of thine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If indeed the salvation of others had been prejudicial to thy advantages, thy grieving might have been reasonable; though not even then would it have been so to one who had learned true wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if thy reward is not increased by the punishment of another, nor diminished by his welfare, why dost thou bewail thyself because that other is freely saved?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As I said, thou oughtest not, even wert thou (one) of the approved, to be pained at the salvation which cometh to the Gentiles through grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when thou, who art guilty before thy Lord of the same things as they, and hast thyself offended, art displeased at the good of others, and thinkest great things, as if thou alone oughtest to be partaker of the grace, thou art guilty not only of envy and insolence, but of extreme folly, and mayest be liable to all the severest torments; for thou hast planted within thyself the root of all evils, pride.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore a wise man has said, “Pride is the beginning of sin” ( Ecclus. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 13 ): that is, its root, its source, its mother.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By this the first created was banished from that happy abode: by this the devil who deceived him had fallen from that height of dignity; from which that accursed one, knowing that the nature of the sin was sufficient to cast down even from heaven itself, came this way when he labored to bring down Adam from such high honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For having puffed him up with the promise that he should be as a God, so he broke him down, and cast him down into the very gulfs of hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because nothing so alienates men from the lovingkindness of God, and gives them over to the fire of the pit, as the tyranny of pride.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when this is present with us, our whole life becomes impure, even though we fulfill temperance, chastity, fasting, prayer, almsgiving, anything.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, “Every one,” saith the wise man, “that is proud in heart is an abomination to the Lord.” ( Prov. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then restrain this swelling of the soul, let us cut up by the roots this lump of pride, if at least we would wish to be clean, and to escape the punishment appointed for the devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that the proud must fall under the same punishment as that (wicked) one, hear Paul declare; “Not a novice, lest being lifted up with pride, he fall into the judgment, and the snare of the devil.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is “the judgment”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He means, into the same “condemnation,” the same punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then does he say, that a man may avoid this dreadful thing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By reflecting upon his own nature, upon the number of his sins, upon the greatness of the torments in that place, upon the transitory nature of the things which seem bright in this world, differing in nothing from grass, and more fading than the flowers of spring.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If we continually stir within ourselves these considerations, and keep in mind those who have walked most upright, the devil, though he strive ten thousand ways, will not be able to lift us up, nor even to trip us at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: May the God who is the God of the humble, the good and merciful God, grant both to you and me a broken and humbled heart, so shall we be enabled easily to order the rest aright, to the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost, be glory forever and ever.
